message:
test(fcp-conformance/planner): pin lease-causality + filter-ordering invariants (br-sesuw)

ExecutionPlanner has documented causality properties that link held
leases and excluded-nodes filters to ranking outcomes. Inline tests
cover happy paths but not the time-domain causality of expired leases
or the filter-ordering invariant that prevents wasted scoring on
excluded nodes.

Adds 9 tests against the public fcp_mesh::planner API:

- expired_lease_does_not_contribute_to_active_load_penalty (lease
  whose expires_at <= current_time MUST NOT bias the score; without
  this a once-leased node would carry "ghost load" forever)
- active_lease_does_contribute_to_load_penalty (causal counterpart:
  an active lease MUST measurably bias against the holder)
- singleton_writer_holder_is_only_eligible_node
- singleton_lease_conflict_reason_names_the_holder (the rejected
  candidate's DecisionReason MUST carry the holder so callers can
  re-route instead of fail)
- excluded_nodes_never_appear_in_ranking (filter-ordering: even a
  high-fitness excluded node never appears)
- plan_is_deterministic_under_fixed_inputs (no time-of-day side
  effects)
- rank_one_candidate_carries_selected_as_best_reason (explainability
  contract)
- empty_node_input_yields_empty_plan (no synthesized entries)
- select_best_returns_first_of_plan (consistency between the two
  public entry points)

All 9 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
planner_lease_causality_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
